scores.correctness_notes: The response describes v4 of the library with different classes and events than the gold standard (v1). The gold standard focuses on interface specification/contract (CSRs, revocation, error codes), while the response describes a completely different API version with different events (CertificateAvailableEvent vs certificate_available/certificate_expiring/certificate_invalidated), different classes (TLSCertificatesRequiresV4), and different renewal mechanisms (secret expiration). The install/import instructions are also wrong.
scores.hallucination_notes: The described API (TLSCertificatesRequiresV4, CertificateAvailableEvent, renew_certificate(), regenerate_private_key(), renewal_relative_time) may exist in v4 but is not what the gold standard describes. The gold standard explicitly notes that specific event names are part of the Python API not the interface spec. The response confidently describes a v4 API that differs substantially from the v1 spec in the gold standard — this is a version mismatch rather than pure hallucination, but the content doesn't match the expected answer.
